Acceptance Criteria: No blind spots on field edges

Expected Results: Model detects rucks and lineouts anywhere on the field

Post Conditions: All relevant events are detected

Test Approach: Model restructuring and comparitive performance test
Test Data: Rugby footage with action across full field
Test Environment: Yolo model in python (combined vs. separate approach
Test Steps: 1. Run rugby.pt on full field footage, 2. Evaluate ruck and lineout detection

Acceptance Criteria: False positives minimized through team identification
Actual Results: Initially flagged all players in the area; fixed with team based colour filtering
Expected Results: Only players from the correct team are flagged when offside
Feature: Offside player detection
Post Conditions: Players identified and marked appropriately
Pre Conditions: Offside zone calculated; player detections running
Test Data: Rugby frames with players in offside zones
Test Environment: Python, OpenCV, team colour classification logic
Test Steps: 1. Run offside detection on player positions, check flagged players

Acceptance Criteria: 100% of non-players are omitted from detection.
Actual Results: No bounding box drawn on referees or other non-payers
Expected Results: Every detected player is classified either into team 1 or 2. No other person is detected.
Feature: Verify that referees and other nonplayer objects are omitted from detection
Post Conditions: There is no bounding box around non-players and user can clearly see what team each player belongs to
Pre Conditions: Team colours of the two playing teams are specified
Test Environment: Windows + python + detection and classification model
Test Steps: 1. Run detection script, 2. Observe whether referees and orhter non-players are detected or omitted

Acceptance Criteria: Players are correctly classified into team based on jersey colour.
Actual Results: Players correctly detected and classified by jersey colour with expected accuracy
Expected Results: Program detects dominant colour of each players jersey and classifies into 1 of 2 teams. Bounding box around the player is the same colour as dominant colour of players jersey
Feature: Verify that colour detection is accurate due to dominant colour and classification functions working correctly
Post Conditions: User is able to clearly see which team each player belongs to.
Pre Conditions: Team colours of the two playing teams are specified. Number of k clusters and brightness are specified.
Test Steps: 1. Run the colour detection script, 2. Input the video with known team jersey colours, 3. Observe classification results
